front: Building: ovoid skyscraper at 30 St Mary Axe in London nicknamed 'the Gherkin'; Swiss Re is the primary occupant; features a diagrid structural facade
back: 30 St Mary Axe / The Gherkin (Norman Foster)

front: Building: built on the ruins of the Baltic Exchange in London
back: 30 St Mary Axe / The Gherkin (Norman Foster)

front: Building: added a glass dome to this parliament building after German reunification; inside, a mirror-paneled cone surrounded by two helical ramps automatically adjusts to control sunlight
Building: Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped this building before its 1990s reconstruction

Building: Hong Kong bank headquarters — exterior supported by eight steel masts that free up the floor space; two cannon-shaped maintenance cranes on the roof aimed at the bad feng shui from I.M. Pei's nearby Bank of China Tower
Building: 'coathanger' trusses suspend groups of floors called 'social villages'; computer-controlled 'sun scoops' reflect sunlight into the atrium
Building: a pair of bronze lions at the main entry — replicas of those outside HSBC Shanghai — are touched by visitors for good luck
Building: Manhattan tower built atop a 1928 cast-stone base by Joseph Urban; 'diagrid' facade with concave corner windows known as 'bird's mouths'
Building: world's tallest bridge; spans the Tarn River gorge in France; designed with engineer Michel Virlogeux
Building: circular office campus in Cupertino nicknamed 'the Spaceship'; co-designed with Apple's Jony Ive; modeled on the quad at Stanford
Architect: British pioneer of high-tech architecture; met Richard Rogers at Yale; together with their future spouses, formed Team 4 (designed the Reliance Controls factory)
Architect: later founded Foster + Partners, Britain's largest architectural firm
Architect: Astana, Kazakhstan — Palace of Peace and Reconciliation (pyramid with 62x62m base, stained-glass apex, four 'hands of peace,' inspired by a congress of world religions)
Architect: Astana, Kazakhstan — Khan Shatyr Entertainment Center (tent-shaped, inspired by yurts, home to the world's largest marquee)
Architect: London City Hall — bulbous shape nicknamed 'The Glass Gonad' designed to minimize the area exposed to direct sunlight
Architect: Masdar City, Abu Dhabi — planned zero-carbon, zero-waste arcological city
Architect: Torre de Collserola (Barcelona) — guy-wire-supported tower with a hollow concrete shaft of 4.5m diameter supporting a ten-story pod
Architect: his Willis Building in London sits across the street from Richard Rogers' Lloyd's Building
Architect: won the Pritzker Prize in 1999; designed the new Wembley Stadium
